data.description:
# Claim-gate <-> plan-review ref-resolution coherence (shared boundary, ADR 0002)

## Why

Plan-review binds the `file_impact` `{path:hash}` map AT the pinned SHA (ADR 0002); the
claim-gate freshness re-check must hash those paths at the SAME ref basis. If the two gates
resolve the ref independently (two hash sites), they can diverge (whole-HEAD vs pinned-SHA),
re-introducing the staleness false-positive ADR 0002 set out to prevent. Split from the
attested-signing pin so each is a thin, independently-reviewable slice.

## What

- Introduce ONE shared ref-resolution boundary consumed by BOTH the signing/plan-review gate
  and the claim-gate freshness re-check — not two independently-coded resolution sites.
- Update ADR 0002 to record the consolidation (and the manifest-channel binding cross-ref).
- Rule-of-Three / YAGNI check: there are exactly two consumers today (plan-review hashing +
  claim-gate re-check); the consolidation is justified by the divergence risk above and is
  de-risked by an explicit back-out.
- Back-out: the shared boundary is reversible to the prior per-site resolution (a flag/revert),
  stated explicitly.

## Scope

- In: the shared ref-resolution boundary, the claim-gate freshness re-check wired through it,
  ADR 0002 update, the back-out path.
- Out: the attested-signing manifest pin (sibling story); snapshot/cache (S1/S2).

## Acceptance Criteria

- [ ] Plan-review `file_impact` hashing and the claim-gate freshness re-check resolve the ref
      through ONE shared boundary (same pinned-SHA basis) — not two hash sites.
- [ ] A negative test asserts both gates hash at the SAME ref (guards whole-HEAD divergence),
      asserting the behavioral contract rather than snapshotting current output.
- [ ] ADR 0002 is updated to record the shared-resolution consolidation.
- [ ] The consolidation has a documented back-out to the prior per-site resolution (reversible).
data.title: Claim-gate <-> plan-review ref-resolution coherence (shared boundary, ADR 0002)
